Monumente

Discover History Around You

Every corner
hides a story

Monumente uses your location to reveal monuments and points of interest within 500 metres — then brings them to life with Wikipedia, AI-generated histories and audio narration.

Available on App Store
Get it on Google Play
Scroll to explore

Features

Everything you need to
explore your surroundings

Six powerful tools, always in your pocket, ready to transform any walk into a journey through time.

Geolocation Radar

Automatically detects monuments and points of interest within 500 metres of your current position in real time.

Directions with Google Maps

Get turn-by-turn directions to any nearby monument in one tap, powered by Google Maps.

Wikipedia Descriptions

Read detailed information about each monument, sourced directly from Wikipedia and always up to date.

AI-Generated History

Ask our AI to uncover the full historical story of any monument — legends, architecture, curiosities and more.

Audio Narration

Both Wikipedia descriptions and AI stories can be read aloud — perfect for hands-free exploration while walking.

Share on Social

Instantly share any nearby monument to Instagram, WhatsApp, X and more — one tap, done.

How it works

Three steps to
unlock the past

1

Open the app

Grant location access once and Monumente instantly starts scanning your surroundings for monuments within 500 metres.

2

Choose a monument

Browse nearby points of interest on the list or map, tap one to see its description, distance and quick actions.

3

Explore & share

Read or listen to the story, get directions, or share the discovery with friends — all without leaving the app.

Stories to discover

Share the wonder

Tell the world what
history surrounds you

Monumente makes it effortless to share a monument discovery with a beautifully formatted card. One tap and your friends can see exactly what you found and where.

Choose from all major platforms: Instagram, WhatsApp, X, Telegram and more.

AI-Powered Stories

History narrated
by artificial intelligence

Wikipedia descriptions are just the beginning. Ask Monumente's AI to dig deeper — uncover legends, architectural secrets, hidden anecdotes and the real story behind every stone.

AI · Generating story

"The Colosseum, known in antiquity as the Flavian Amphitheatre, was commissioned by Emperor Vespasian around 70 AD on the site of a former artificial lake. Capable of holding up to 80,000 spectators, it stands as a testament to Roman engineering genius — its hypogeum, a network of underground tunnels, could raise wild animals directly into the arena through a system of trapdoors..."

Listen

today
